Spice e-Auction Platform

Run every e-Auction day with confidence.

From lot entry to ledger — branch allocation, GST invoices, RTGS payments, trade reports and Tally export, all on one floor.

  • Lot entry with branch allocation & live validation
  • One-click GST invoices, debit notes & PDFs
  • RTGS bank payment files & receipts
  • Trade reports, journals & Tally XML export — no re-keying

💾 Backup & Restore

Download the entire database as a single .db file, or restore from one you saved earlier. A backup captures every auction, lot, invoice, payment, debit note, setting and user — bit-for-bit identical to what's running right now.

Download Backup
Saves a timestamped spice-eauction-backup-YYYY-MM-DD…db file to your downloads folder. Take one before any major change, or on a regular schedule.
🕒Auto Backup Schedule Recommended
Automatically download a fresh backup at a fixed interval. Keep this browser tab open during business hours so the schedule can fire. Last run timestamp is shown below.
Not scheduled
⚠️Restore from Backup
Replaces ALL current data with the contents of the uploaded file. Anything not in the backup will be lost. Download a backup first if you want a recovery point.
🗑Delete All — wipe an entire table
Each button wipes every row from the named table (ignoring any filters elsewhere in the app). The server snapshots the database to data/backups/before-delete-…<timestamp>.db first, so a misclick is recoverable via Restore above. Every wipe is audit-logged.
📜 Audit log — recent wipes
Shows the 200 most recent Delete All actions.
Click Refresh to load recent wipes.

Add New User

Create a login for another person (e.g. your dad, or a branch operator). They'll use it from their own device.

3–30 characters · letters, digits, . _ - only
Share this with the person — they can change it later from their account.
Can create/edit lots, generate invoices, and manage buyers/traders. Cannot delete records or change settings.

🔑 Change your password

You're signed in with a default password. Pick a new one before continuing — it'll be needed on your next sign-in from any device.

Reset User Password

Resetting 's password will immediately sign them out from all devices.

Change Role

Update 's permissions. The change takes effect on their next request — they don't need to sign out.

Lot Reassignment Requests

Mobile operators request lot-number ranges to be moved between branches. Approving performs the reassignment immediately (same as a manual reassign) and re-checks that no lot in the range is already booked. The operator is notified in their app.

▼ Pre-auction Exports Download BEFORE importing prices — your "before auction" snapshot

Lot Slip, Lot Buyer, Lot Name, e-Auction CSV, Price List (Before) and Dealer List — your pre-import safety net. Run them now while the lots table still reflects the auction sheet.

▼ Export Center — XLSX

🔒
Export Center locked
The post-auction Export Center unlocks once Price Import has been run for this trade. Import the auctioneer's price sheet from Lots → Price Import, then return here.

▼ DBF Exports FoxPro-compatible format for legacy integration

Each transaction module exports as .dbf (for the FoxPro application — date format DD/MM/YYYY, lot numbers keep leading zeros) or as .xlsx (same columns, opens in Excel).

Trade-wise

Date-wise

Master Data

Full seller / buyer master — available as Excel (.xlsx) or FoxPro (.dbf).

Lorry Reports Auction-floor reports for the day's auction

Note: All three reports pull live data from the selected auction's lots and the buyers master. The Buyer Lot Lorry report classifies buyers as Intra-state when the buyer's state matches the auction state, otherwise Inter-state.

To Tally Generate Tally-importable XML files from your auction data 🔗 Open Link

Two Tally companies for ledgers: Sales Party Ledgers import buyer-side parties into the Tally company. RD & URD Purchase Party Ledgers import into the Tally company (since suppliers are ASP's). All vouchers currently import into ISP — open the matching company in Tally before importing.
Workflow: Import party ledgers first, then matching vouchers — Tally rejects imports if any referenced ledger is missing.

▼ 📚 Party Ledgers 3

🛒
Sales Party Ledgers
Buyers from this auction's invoices (under Local / Inter Dealer-Purchase)
🏢
RD Purchase Party Ledgers
Registered dealers (under Local / Inter Dealer)
🌾
URD Purchase Party Ledgers
Agriculturists (under Purchase From Agriculturist)

▼ 🎯 Single Party Ledger

Pick one party from this auction and download just its ledger. Useful when you've added a new buyer/trader and only need to create that one ledger in Tally.
🔍
Pick an auction and click Refresh List to load parties.

▼ 🎫 Single Voucher

Generate one voucher XML at a time — handy when you missed an entry or edited one after the bulk import and don't want to re-import the whole trade. Pick By Invoice (sales invoice number) or By Party (buyer / dealer / agriculturist).
Pick a sales invoice and download just that voucher.
🔍
Pick an auction and click Refresh List to load invoices.

▼ 📄 Vouchers 6

💰
Sales Vouchers
External customers — full e-way bill, dispatch + transport details
📥
RD Purchase Vouchers
Registered dealers — input GST, TDS 194Q, batch tracking
🌾
URD Purchase Vouchers
Agriculturist / unregistered — Nil Rated, no GST input
📝
Debit Notes
Discount received from suppliers — 18% GST credit
🌱
Debit Notes - Planter
Discount on planter bills of supply — 18% GST credit

▼ 🗺️ E-way Bill Distance Per-invoice override

ISP sales vouchers carry a <DISTANCE> field used by the e-way bill portal. Click NIC to look up the distance on the official portal (captcha-gated; one route at a time), paste the result, click Save. The value is saved per route, so every other invoice between the same two PINs — this auction and all future ones — picks it up automatically. The auto badge marks rows that resolved via the saved route table.
Pick an auction and click Refresh.
How to import into Tally:
  1. Open your Tally company (must match the name in Settings → To Tally).
  2. Press Alt+O → Import → Vouchers.
  3. Select the downloaded .xml file and confirm.
  4. Tally rejects the import if any ledger name in the XML doesn't exist in your company — make sure the ledgers in Settings → To Tally match your Tally setup exactly.

Journals Sales & Purchase registers (filter by auction)

Registers Lot-wise Purchase & invoice-wise Sales — by auction or date range

Pick an Auction for a single auction, or leave it on “All auctions” and set a From/To date range. The date range is ignored when a specific auction is selected.

Reports — Spice Board

Statutory cardamom-auction reports submitted to the Spices Board (Cardamom Marketing Rules).

Import Old Data Upload legacy XLS/XLSX exports back into the system

Price List Mapping Upload a Price List (Before) sheet with TRADE NAME filled in — server fills the CODE column from the Buyers master

Expected columns: AUCTION NO, DATE, LOT, BAG, QTY, CODE, TRADE NAME  ·  The matcher needs both TRADE NAME and CODE headers somewhere on the sheet. Extra columns are kept as-is.

Price Check Upload a price sheet — server validates the SERVER PRICE column against lots.price in the database

Expected columns: LOT and SERVER PRICE (required)  ·  KERALA/STATE, TNO/ANO, DATE, BAG, QTY, PRICE, CODE (optional, kept for context)  ·  If SERVER PRICE is absent, falls back to PRICE. Pre-pick an auction below to ignore TNO/DATE in the file.

Seller

Add Seller

🏦 Bank Accounts
Add one or more bank accounts. The first bank is used as the default on purchase invoices.

Buyer

Add Buyer

Full buyer code used everywhere
Short alias used in auction tags & price files. Type a prefix (e.g. JPJ) to see existing codes.
Consignee / Ship-To Details (optional)

New Auction

Lot Allocations — Auction #

Click any free chip to mark it for deletion. Used (entered) chips are locked. To add a new range, use + Add Branch.

📋 Auto-fill from existing lots — Use this when a trade was created via import (or otherwise has lots without matching allocations). One click adds a wide range (e.g. 001–999) per branch + lot-prefix combination found in the lots table, so every existing lot has a branch home. Safe to re-run: anything already covered is skipped.

Move unused lot numbers from one branch to another. Click a tile to toggle it, or drag across tiles to sweep a whole range (e.g. 090–099) — drag again elsewhere to add another range. Non-adjacent picks (e.g. 003, 007, 012) work too. Or type a contiguous range below. Booked lots (with sellers) cannot be reassigned.

Free Allocated Booked Reassigned Selected

→

Add a new lot range to the current auction. Existing allocations are preserved.

Generate Sales Invoice(s)

Select an auction first

Generate Purchase Invoice

Only for sellers with GSTIN (registered dealers).

Generate All

When set, only buyers whose default sale type matches are included.
Each generated invoice increments by 1 from here

Commission Bill (Format 2)

Generates a consolidated Commission Bill (Format 2) for the selected auction — one row per seller listing PurAmt, Refund, Commission, Handling, GST and net dues.

Generate Agriculturist Bill of Supply

Only for sellers without GSTIN (farmers, agriculturists). No GST is charged. Sellers with GSTIN should use Generate Purchase Invoice instead.

Generate Debit Note

Generates one debit note for the specified purchase invoice. The invoice must belong to the selected auction.

—

Generate All Debit Notes

Generates one debit note per eligible purchase in the selected auction. Purchases already having a debit note are skipped automatically. No cross-auction data — only purchases under this auction are touched.

—
Loading eligible purchases…

Skipped Debit Notes

These invoices did not produce a debit note. Click a row's reason to filter, or use the search box.

InvoiceSaleAuctionBuyerReason

Invoice Preview — not yet saved

Import Lots from XLS / XLSX

Import lots from an Excel file. The file should have columns like ANO (auction no), DATE, LOT, NAME, BAG, QTY, etc.

💡 If your file has multiple auction numbers (ANO 1, 2, 3…), leave the override fields below blank — each row goes into its own auction automatically.

Price Import — Post-Auction Data

⚠ Download "before auction" snapshots first
Once you import prices the lots table is overwritten with the auctioneer's data. These reports are your record of the pre-auction state. Snapshots use the auction picked in the Lots tab.

Use this after the auction ends to update prices, buyer codes, and quantities on existing lots.

Expected columns: STATE, TNO (or ANO), DATE, LOT, BAG, QTY, PRICE, CODE
Extra columns are ignored. Amount is auto-computed as QTY × PRICE if not provided.
🚫 Validate entered lots first
This trade's entered lots haven't been validated. Run validation and resolve any issues before importing prices.

Validate Entered Lots

Loading…

Edit Purchase Invoice

Edit Bill of Supply

Edit Debit Note

Generate Debit Note - Planter

Generates one debit note for the specified planter bill of supply. The bill must belong to the selected auction.

—

Generate All Debit Notes - Planter

Generates one debit note per eligible planter bill of supply in the selected auction. Bills already having a debit note are skipped automatically. No cross-auction data.

—
